supervisor.reason: The daemon queue/session serialization change is implemented and validated.

bootstrap.goal: Make daemonize create a fresh session for each newly started prompt file and keep later prompt files pending until the earlier prompt is completed.

tasks.resume_checkpoint: Resume from commit finalization and push for the validated daemon queue/session change.

items.text: Phase 1. Inspect current daemon prompt processing, session creation, and queue behavior to identify the right serialization point
Phase 2. Make daemonize process one prompt at a time and keep later ready prompt files pending until the current prompt is completed
Phase 3. Keep `waiting_for_plan` prompts as queue blockers, release the queue when their session `PLAN.md` becomes fully complete, and finalize the result report at release time
Phase 4. Ensure every newly started daemon prompt gets a unique fresh session even when multiple sessions start within the same second
Phase 5. Add regression coverage and rerun daemon plus session-related CLI validation

next_action: Create a commit for the validated daemon queue/session change and push it to the current branch.
notes: Treat this file as machine truth and keep Markdown synchronized.
DASHBOARD.md should show the actual progress of the active scope.
PLAN.md should list prompt-derived development items in `[ ] Phase N. <title>` format.
The installed guidance bundle and packaged asset copy should stay synchronized.
Legacy TASKS.md snapshots should be migrated forward into PLAN.md without losing checklist state.
A new prompt-driven scope now targets daemonized prompt watching from JSON configuration.
The repository does not currently contain .dev/PROJECT.md or .dev/ROADMAP.md, so planning is grounded in the direct request, AGENTS.md, current code, and machine state.
Daemon prompt execution is now serialized so only one prompt file advances at a time and later files remain pending until the active prompt is completed.
Waiting-for-plan prompts now block the daemon queue until their session PLAN.md becomes fully complete, at which point the runner finalizes the result and releases the next prompt.
Generated session IDs now add a numeric suffix when a same-second session directory already exists, preventing daemon prompt files from colliding onto one session.
Focused validation passed through python3 -m unittest tests.test_daemon and python3 -m unittest tests.test_cli tests.test_loop_runner.
